title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: South Boston's St. Patrick's Day Parade began at Andrew Square, with spectators lining the streets on March 15, 2026.
Participants included Kathy Luginbuel and Teresa Rogers from Florida, marching with Tampa Bay's Fascinators group.
Scott Poce of East Bridgewater painted his eyebrows green to celebrate the parade in South Boston.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Two men arrested for cocaine trafficking after investigation into a fatal overdose in Barnstable County, Massachusetts.
William Sarnie and Andre Silvia charged with trafficking over 200 grams of cocaine, held without bail pending hearings.
Investigation involved local and state police, DEA, spanned multiple towns including Nantucket, Sandwich, and Wareham.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and performed as musical guest on SNL's March 14 episode, marking his second time in dual roles.
Ryan Gosling appeared to introduce Styles' performance of 'Dance No More' from the album 'Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally.'
Styles' SNL history includes musical guest performances in 2012, 2013, 2014 with One Direction, and solo in 2017.

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Boston Public Schools teacher John Magee Gavin sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation crimes.
Gavin coerced minors for sexualized online interactions, received child sexual abuse material from victims.
Authorities identified Gavin's Discord account, linking him to 20 underage victims across the US, Canada, and UK.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots eye five free agents including Tyreek Hill, known for speed despite recent leg injury.
Marquise Brown, with 5 touchdowns in 2025, considered for speed and big-play potential.
Linebackers Bobby Okereke and Kenneth Murray evaluated for strengthening Patriots' defense.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta scored 24 points and grabbed 10 rebounds, leading the Celtics to a 111-100 win over the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum struggled with his three-point shooting, hitting only 2 of 9 attempts and shooting 29% from beyond the arc.
Despite shooting woes, Boston held Wizards stars Alex Sarr and Trae Young to a combined 15 points in the game.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys opened their St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert at MGM Music Hall on March 13.
The concert featured performances by openers The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown.
Haywire collaborated with Dropkick Murphys on a split EP titled 'New England Forever'.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to Gotham FC in their NWSL debut at Gillette Stadium.
The match included an NWSL record-tying eight yellow cards, with Legacy's Bianca St-Georges receiving a red card.
Over 30,000 fans attended, marking a strong start for Boston Legacy FC amidst a Boston-New York soccer rivalry.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, 2026, celebrating their 30th anniversary.
The band surprised audiences with political statements and new tracks, including 'Citizen I.C.E.' protesting ICE actions.
Openers included The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, with Dropkick Murphys leading through an energetic setlist.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at age 11, Bennett wants to support the Joslin Diabetes Center through his marathon effort.
He aims to inspire others with diabetes by proving a diagnosis doesn't limit one's ability to achieve athletic goals.
